skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
skill.summary: Design polished frontend interfaces with distinctive visual direction and production-ready implementation guidance.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Official documentation-only skill with frontend design guidance in SKILL.md. No scripts, commands, network calls, filesystem access, or environment access were found. Static review found no prompt injection attempt or dangerous pattern.

content.value_statement: Generic interface prompts often produce flat layouts, common fonts, and weak visual identity. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward polished frontend work with a clear aesthetic direction.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of web components, pages, applications, and interface screens.
Encourages a clear aesthetic direction before implementation begins.
Promotes distinctive typography, color systems, motion, and spatial composition.
Supports HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, and comparable frontend stacks.
Provides guidance for CSS animations, hover states, and refined visual details.
Helps avoid generic AI interface patterns and repeated default styles.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not execute or verify generated code.
It does not replace accessibility, performance, or cross-browser testing.
It does not create native Figma, Sketch, or image design source files.
Results still depend on the user brief, assets, framework, and project constraints.

use_cases.title: Build Memorable Components
use_cases.description: Create polished buttons, cards, navigation, dashboards, and interaction states with a strong visual direction.

use_cases.title: Prototype Product Screens
use_cases.description: Turn a product idea into working interface code with typography, color, layout, and motion decisions.

use_cases.title: Upgrade Plain Interfaces
use_cases.description: Refine an existing page so it feels intentional, cohesive, and less like a default template.

prompt_templates.title: Create One Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a [component name] for [user goal]. Use [framework] and choose a clear aesthetic direction that fits [audience].
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a focused interface element.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Page Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a [section type] for [product or service]. Include distinctive typography, color, spacing, and hover states.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a reusable landing page or app section.

prompt_templates.title: Create An Interactive Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [screen type] with [core workflow]. Add motion, responsive layout, and refined empty, loading, and active states.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for an interface with states and interactions.

prompt_templates.title: Transform An Existing UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign this [existing interface description] for [brand tone]. Keep the behavior, but improve visual hierarchy and originality.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for redesigning an existing product surface.

output_examples.input: Create a finance dashboard with a refined industrial style.
output: Dense dashboard layout with clear scan paths and restrained contrast.
Monospace numeric treatments, compact controls, and deliberate accent colors.
Responsive panels, active states, and subtle motion for important changes.

output_examples.input: Design a landing section for a boutique music app.
output: A distinctive hero section with editorial typography and immersive media treatment.
A cohesive palette, tactile controls, and audio-inspired motion details.
Responsive spacing and supporting sections that keep the product visible.

output_examples.input: Improve a plain settings page for a developer tool.
output: Organized controls with clear grouping, labels, and predictable interaction patterns.
Sharper hierarchy using spacing, icon buttons, tabs, and compact form states.
A quieter visual system suited to repeated professional use.

best_practices: State the audience, product goal, framework, and desired tone before asking for implementation.
Ask for responsive states, interaction states, and accessibility considerations in the same request.
Provide brand assets, screenshots, or constraints when the design must match an existing product.
anti_patterns: Requesting a beautiful UI without giving context, users, product purpose, or technical stack.
Accepting common default fonts, purple gradients, and generic card layouts without revision.
Treating visual polish as complete before testing responsiveness, accessibility, and real content.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create frontend components, pages, applications, and interface screens with stronger visual direction.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it write production code?
faq.answer: It guides the assistant to produce functional frontend code, but users should still review and test the output.

faq.question: Can it work with React or Vue?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill mentions React, Vue, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and similar frontend approaches.

faq.question: Does it include security-sensitive behavior?
faq.answer: No. The skill is markdown guidance only and contains no executable scripts or external service calls.

faq.question: How should I get the best result?
faq.answer: Give a clear user goal, target audience, framework, brand tone, and any required interaction states.
